subject: LU-9859 libcfs: don't stomp on module string for cfs_str2num_check

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Cannot build patch due to 14, messages:

  /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/libcfs/libcfs/libcfs_string.c: In function ‘cfs_str2num_check’:
 /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/libcfs/libcfs/libcfs_string.c:290:8: error: assignment makes pointer from integer without a cast [-Werror]
   cache = *endp;
         ^
 /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/libcfs/libcfs/libcfs_string.c:294:8: error: assignment makes integer from pointer without a cast [-Werror]
   *endp = cache;
         ^
 /home/green/smt/git/lustre-release/libcfs/libcfs/libcfs_string.c: At top level:
 cc1: error: unrecognized command line option "-Wno-format-truncation" [-Werror]

parents.subject: LU-16785 build: Cleanup test for IS_ENCRYPTED macro

commit.subject: LU-9859 libcfs: don't stomp on module string for cfs_str2num_check
commit.message:
LU-9859 libcfs: don't stomp on module string for cfs_str2num_check

The update to cfs_str2num_check() reintrouded strim() which was
removed since it was breaking the root squash feature (LU-1778).
Also trim() is directly being executed on the module parameter
string which is wrong.

Change-Id: I4fa3c65a01362dc01b52cd17b344c358ddecc9ce
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
